metadata.title: Projetos-SafeLabs submodule → XPL-Forge integration map
metadata.scope_note: Survey of superproject .gitmodules (GitHub-facing paths). Azure DevOps trees (Safelabs-Harpia, Mantis, etc.) are excluded from code merge — mirror or policy review first.

forge_routerxpl.summary: Embedded / SOHO router, switch, TAP, CPE exploitation and cred scanners.

high_fit.notes: Semantic ancestor; diff for vendor modules and PoC alignment.
RouterOS API abuse patterns → creds/scanners under Mikrotik class.
Already ingested style in third_party_router_exploit_repos / PoC indexes — do not bulk-merge foreign code.
Cross-link CVE and exploit metadata into extended catalogs.
Wordlist packs for scoped cred tests (respect SecLists license/attribution).
Offline PCAP workflows adjacent to cred/session heuristics for router contexts.
Third-party IoT exploit pack — intel only unless license cleared.

low_or_reference.notes: Brute patterns; different codebase — extract wordlist/protocol ideas only with attribution.
Printer class explicitly out_of_scope in module_target_scope; keep external.

forge_wirelessxpl.summary: 802.11 / WPA3 / BLE lab shell, PCAP tooling, offline analysis.

high_fit.notes: Research repos (WPA3, deauth, evil twin writeups) → wifi_lab / catalogs / wiki references; no wholesale copy.
Shared PCAP pipeline DNA with WirelessXPL offline modules.
CVE PoC listing — extend cve_extended_catalog / ingest tools.

low_or_reference.notes: Few wireless-adjacent modules; primary home remains RouterXPL-Forge.

forge_firewallxpl.summary: NGFW, UTM, WAF, cloud perimeter lab.

high_fit.notes: Purple-team orchestration — optional adapter for lab chain, not direct exploit merge.
Atomic tests for validation of detections on perimeter stacks.

low_or_reference.notes: ICS exploit framework — wrong trust model for NGFW core; keep OT-separated.
Defensive lab — correlate rules with firewall logging tests only.

excluded_azure_or_corporate.notes: Do not publish or merge into public forges without contractual mirror and redaction.

ot_cluster_general.notes: OT/ICS belongs to separate arsenal; FirewallXPL may reference perimeter segmentation narratives only.
